symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic diffuse bone pain (back, pelvis, legs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse bone pain is a cardinal feature of osteomalacia.
Diffuse bone pain in an older adult requires malignancy to be excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Proximal muscle weakness with difficulty rising and waddling gait

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vitamin-D-related proximal myopathy causes a waddling gait and difficulty rising.
Proximal girdle symptoms can overlap, but PMR does not cause bony tenderness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Graded proximal power 3/5 in hip flexors and shoulder abductors with preserved distal strength

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective symmetrical proximal myopathy — a clinician-measured sign that the AI cannot grade remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-impact fall with inability to weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to weight-bear after a fall requires imaging to exclude fracture.
Proximal myopathy plus softened bone predisposes to falls and insufficiency fractures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bony tenderness on firm palpation of sternum and anterior tibiae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible bony (not muscular) tenderness is characteristic; distinguishes from PMR. Requires palpation the AI cannot perform.
PMR produces muscular stiffness, not exquisite bony tenderness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dietary deprivation — low dairy and fortified foods, poor intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor dietary vitamin D and calcium intake is a major contributor to deficiency osteomalacia.
Lack of cutaneous synthesis from sunlight is a leading cause of vitamin D deficiency in housebound older adults.
Muscle cramps/spasms can reflect hypocalcaemia from vitamin D deficiency; screen for tetany features.

symptoms.symptom_name: No weight loss, night sweats or fevers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of constitutional B-symptoms lowers (but does not exclude) malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bladder/bowel continence intact; no saddle anaesthesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative sphincter and saddle screen makes cauda equina unlikely, but it must be safety-netted.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Vitamin D deficiency not confirmed and replacement not correctly initiated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low 25-OH vitamin D confirms deficiency — a lab test unavailable in telehealth.

loinc_ref.display: Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A key marker of active osteomalacia is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised ALP reflects increased osteoblast activity in unmineralised bone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Looser zones / pseudofractures on plain radiography

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain radiographs of pelvis and femurs
investigation_details.expected_result: Looser zones (pseudofractures); exclude acute displaced fracture
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Osteomalacia radiological hallmark missed and an occult acute fracture not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Looser zones are characteristic of osteomalacia; imaging is unavailable in telehealth.
Radiographs are required to exclude an acute fracture behind the inability to weight-bear.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Diffuse bone pain, proximal muscle weakness, bony tenderness on palpation, and elevated ALP (with Looser zones) together are the classic tetrad of osteomalacia — but the palpation, power grading, ALP and radiograph are all clinician/lab-obtained and unavailable remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiographs of pelvis and bilateral femurs
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude an acute occult insufficiency / neck-of-femur fracture behind the inability to weight-bear, and look for Looser zones.
Confirm vitamin D deficiency to guide replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Calcium, phosphate, ALP, PTH (metabolic bone panel)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise the metabolic bone disease and screen for hypocalcaemia and secondary hyperparathyroidism.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Malignancy screen (FBC, ESR/CRP, EUC, serum/urine electrophoresis)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude myeloma / metastatic bone disease as a cause of diffuse bone pain in an older adult.
